name: manager-first-90
description: New manager's first 90 days. Prepare for meetings, build an onboarding guide for yourself, analyze team feedback, plan your days, and establish a weekly review cadence. Lead from day one.

config.instruction: Prepare for your first round of 1:1s with each direct report. Questions to ask: what's working, what's broken, what would you change if you were me, what does success look like for you, and what should I know that nobody will tell me? Also prep for your first meeting with your own boss.

Build your own onboarding guide as a new manager: who does what on the team, current projects and status, key stakeholders, team dynamics and history, processes and tools, upcoming deadlines, and the last 3 things that went wrong.

After the 1:1s, synthesize the feedback. Identify: common themes (what everyone agrees on), contradictions (where people disagree), quick wins (easy fixes that build trust), and systemic issues (things only a manager can fix). Prioritize by impact.

Build your management routine: daily standup or check-in cadence, weekly 1:1 schedule, time for strategic thinking (not just firefighting), office hours for the team, and blocks for your own deliverables. Leave slack — management is interrupt-driven.

Establish your weekly review cadence: what to review (team velocity, blockers, morale signals, stakeholder requests), what to communicate up (to your boss), what to communicate down (to your team), and what to adjust. Include a 30/60/90 day milestone tracker.
